title: parathelypteris adscendens
intro: Parathelypteris adscendens, also known as the climbing lady fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae. It is native to parts of Asia and has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The plant is known to have several health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating coughs, and treating respiratory issues. The herbal preparations of Parathelypteris adscendens include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which can be used to treat various health conditions, making it a valuable plant in traditional medicine.
